name: result_exceeds_input
code:
proof fn phi_1_result_exceeds_input(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) > a,
{
}
reason: Aligning down should never produce a value strictly greater than the original input

name: zero_aligns_to_nonzero
code:
proof fn phi_2_zero_aligns_to_nonzero(b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(0usize, b) != 0usize,
{
}
reason: Zero is a multiple of every positive integer so aligning zero must yield zero

name: result_not_multiple_of_alignment
code:
proof fn phi_3_result_not_multiple_of_alignment(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a <= usize::MAX,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) % b != 0,
{
}
reason: The whole purpose of alignment is to produce a multiple of b; failing that means the spec is wrong

name: not_idempotent
code:
proof fn phi_4_not_idempotent(a: usize, b: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
    ensures
        align_to_usize(a, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Aligning a value that is already a multiple of b should be a no-op; changing it indicates a broken spec

name: offset_within_block_differs
code:
proof fn phi_5_offset_within_block_differs(a: usize, b: usize, offset: usize)
    requires
        b > 0,
        a % b == 0,
        0 < offset < b,
        a as int + offset as int <= usize::MAX as int,
    ensures
        align_to_usize((a + offset) as usize, b) != a,
{
}
reason: Any address within the same aligned block should round down to the block base; disagreement reveals a spec error
